source: Eritrea Ministry of Health Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI); WHO/UNICEF JRF 2024 country profile; WHO AFRO Eritrea; UNICEF Eritrea country pages; Gavi Eritrea
source_notes: Schedule composed from WHO/UNICEF JRF Eritrea 2024 country profile, WHO AFRO Eritrea, Gavi Eritrea, and the WHO Immunization Data portal for Eritrea (ERI). WHO-aligned 6/10/14-week primary series. Eritrea introduced PCV (2014), Rotavirus (2014), and IPV at 14 weeks. HepB birth dose is NOT routine in public EPI — HepB delivered via Pentavalent only. Eritrea historically has high routine coverage (often >95% national) compared to neighbours. MCV2 introduced at 18 months. YF and MenA subnational depending on risk.
verification_notes: Phase 1 seed marked needs_review on the country selector. Do NOT treat as clinically authoritative until row-level reconciliation against the current WHO/XMart 2024 JRF schedule and Eritrea MoH EPI bulletin is complete. Open items include: HepB birth dose introduction status, current PCV product (PCV10 vs PCV13), MR (Measles-Rubella) integration timing, MCV2 timing variations, YF subnational risk classification.
source_year_or_release_date: 2024 WHO/UNICEF JRF schedule data; seed first added 2026-04-25
scope: Routine childhood immunisation, birth to 72 months (6 years). Compulsory (Eritrea EPI) and subnationally-routine antigens.
context_note: Source-country schedule. In cross-border reconciliation INTO Egypt, Eritrea-EPI doses are the reliable signal on an Eritrean card (which may use Tigrinya, Arabic, English, or Italian). Eritrea public EPI does NOT give a routine HepB birth dose — HepB is delivered via Pentavalent only. An Eritrean-origin child with documented Pentavalent ×3 has completed their HepB course (3 doses). Yellow Fever (subnational) is Eritrea-routine where given but not on the Egyptian schedule — preserve on the record but do not count as 'missing' for Egypt.
key_differences_vs_egypt: Eritrea uses WHO-aligned 6/10/14-week primary series; Egypt uses 2/4/6-month series
Eritrea uses Pentavalent (DTPw-Hib-HepB, 5-in-1, no IPV); Egypt uses Hexavalent (6-in-1, includes IPV)
Eritrea public EPI does NOT give a routine HepB birth dose — HepB is delivered via Pentavalent starting at 6 weeks; Egypt gives HepB monovalent at birth
Eritrea gives separate IPV at 14 weeks; Egypt's IPV is bundled in Hexavalent
Eritrea gives Measles monovalent at 9 months and a second measles-containing dose at 18 months; Egypt gives MMR at 12 months and 18 months
Eritrea has routine Rotavirus (Rota1 6w, Rota2 10w) in EPI; Egypt does not
Eritrea has routine PCV at 6/10/14 weeks; Egypt PCV is recommended/private only
Eritrea gives Yellow Fever in YF-endemic regions; Egypt does not (YF non-endemic)

doses.notes: At birth or as soon as possible after birth.
OPV0 at birth. Eritrea uses bivalent OPV (bOPV) in routine schedule.
OPV1 at 6 weeks. Co-administered with Pentavalent, PCV, Rotavirus.
Penta1 at 6 weeks. 5-in-1 covering DTPw, Hib, HepB. This is the FIRST HepB exposure in Eritrea public EPI — no routine HepB birth dose.
PCV1 at 6 weeks. Eritrea uses PCV (product label varies — historically PCV13 from procurement).
Rota1 at 6 weeks. 2-dose oral series. Series must be initiated by 15 weeks and completed by 24 months per WHO.
Rota2 at 10 weeks. Series complete after dose 2.
Penta3 at 14 weeks. Primary DTPw-Hib-HepB series complete.
IPV1 at 14 weeks. Single fIPV dose; second IPV under WHO consideration.
PCV3 at 14 weeks. Primary PCV series complete.
Measles first dose (MCV1) at 9 months. Eritrea public EPI delivers measles-monovalent at 9 months (not MMR).
Yellow Fever at 9 months in YF-endemic regions. WHO YF certificate is lifetime-valid.
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF Eritrea 2024; WHO YF country list

doses.notes: Measles second dose (MCV2) at 18 months. May appear as 'MR2' on newer cards where Measles-Rubella has been integrated.

open_questions: HepB birth dose introduction status — currently not routine in public EPI.
Current PCV product (PCV10 vs PCV13) — verify against most recent procurement.
MR (Measles-Rubella) integration at MCV2 — varies by year.
